raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a phone call at work from an employee at Barclays Credit card company about suspicious, fraudulent activity on my credit card. I immediately checked my phone application Barclay 's Account while on the line with the employee and notice two separate charges from XXXX in XXXX, Massachusetts. The employee asked me if I made these purchases. I said no and wanted those charges flagged as fraudulent. The company immediately flagged the charges as fraudulent and removed them from my account. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, Barclays sent me an email stating that they found the charges to be valid as " the card was identified as being in your possession and chip card technology was used, which can not be counterfeited '' I then called them back to dispute this and asked them to re-investigate. They told me to fax them supporting documents. I faxed them the following documents : A copy of driving location records from an application I have on my phone confirming my whereabouts in XXXX, Massachusetts the whole entire day of the crime, a previous police report and XXXX XXXX claim from a couple months prior as there was fraudulent activity on my credit report, and a letter from my supervisor confirming my attendance at my place of employment from the hours of XXXX until XXXX along with my timesheet from my place of employment. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, Barclays sent me a follow-up email stating that after an investigation the fraudulent charges are NOT fraudulent, and I would be responsible for the charges, and those charges re-appeared on my credit card bill. 
I called them on Monday XX/XX/XXXX and told them I would like them to re-investigate because I didn't make these charges and I don't want to be held responsible for something I didn't buy. They told me they would re-investigate, but I never got a follow-up letter and the charges are still on my credit card bill. Now my credit report has a new remark from Barclays stating that " the customer disputes after resolution '', which has changed my credit score by XXXX points. 
I have had an amazing credit score for a long while. I don't have a criminal record. I pay all my payments on time and keep a low balance. This " technology '' they're saying can not be counterfeited is despicable. I do a lot of online shopping, I use my card at gas pumps and grocery stores. Someone, or some group of hackers could have easily stolen my information from anywhere.
